name: Reputation Gating
description: A narrative or systemic gate that unlocks options based on reputation, faction standing, or social state.
design_notes: Reputation gating turns player history into access rules. It should be transparent enough for players to understand consequences while preserving story texture and surprise.
design_purpose: Make social choices affect available quests, dialogue, vendors, or areas.
Represent faction trust or hostility in world systems.
Reward or restrict play styles through accumulated reputation.
player_fantasy: The player feels their history with people and factions has practical consequences.

parameters.name: faction_id
parameters.description: Faction or relationship track used by the gate.

parameters.name: bypass_condition_ids
parameters.description: Alternate story, item, or skill conditions that bypass the gate.

parameters.name: reputation_delta_on_choice
parameters.description: Standing change caused by a relevant choice.

edge_cases: Players can belong to hostile factions with conflicting gates.
Temporary disguises or story states may override reputation.
Content patches can move thresholds and affect existing saves.
common_bugs: UI hides a locked option without explaining the reputation requirement.
Negative reputation wraps around or uses the wrong faction key.
Bypass conditions unlock content but do not mark required story flags.
balancing_notes: Hard gates make choices consequential but can hide too much content.
Soft gates with alternate routes preserve agency while still honoring reputation.
accessibility_notes: Explain locked options in plain language and avoid color-only faction standing indicators.

implementation_notes.unity: Store faction standings and gate definitions as data, then evaluate the same condition object for dialogue display, quest activation, vendors, and save migration.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent gates as Resources or dictionaries with faction_id, threshold, and bypass conditions, resolved through a central narrative-state service.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Gameplay Tags, Data Assets, or quest data tables for faction IDs and evaluate reputation conditions in dialogue, vendor, and level-access systems.
implementation_notes.web: Keep reputation tracks and gate conditions in JSON, run pure condition checks for both locked-option display and activation, and include bypass reasons in UI data.

example_games.title: Fallout: New Vegas
example_games.note: Uses faction reputation to alter reactions and access to content.
